channel.title: BPS Bali on Bali Kini
channel.description: Recent content in BPS Bali on Bali Kini

item.title: BPS Bali Finds Anomaly: Foreign Tourists Down 6%, Hotels Fuller Than Ever
item.description:
Bali’s Central Statistics Agency (BPS) has flagged an unusual phenomenon in the island’s tourism landscape for April 2026: the number of foreign tourist (wisman) arrivals fell on a year-on-year basis, yet hotel room occupancy rates rose. The anomaly raises new questions about the quality-versus-quantity debate at the heart of Bali’s tourism strategy.

April 2026 Tourist Data: Monthly Rebound, Annual Decline

According to the official BPS Bali release, foreign tourists arriving directly in Bali in April 2026 totalled 553,328 visits. Month-on-month, that figure rose 17.21 percent compared to March 2026 — a seasonal rebound typical of the lead-up to the Northern Hemisphere summer.

item.title: BPS Bali Temukan Anomali: Wisman Turun 6%, Hotel Justru Makin Penuh
item.description:
Badan Pusat Statistik (BPS) Provinsi Bali mengungkapkan sebuah fenomena tak biasa pada pariwisata Pulau Dewata di bulan April 2026: jumlah kunjungan wisatawan mancanegara (wisman) secara tahunan justru mengalami penurunan, namun tingkat hunian kamar (TPK) hotel malah melonjak. Anomali ini memicu pertanyaan baru soal kualitas versus kuantitas pariwisata Bali.

Data Wisman April 2026: Naik Bulanan, Turun Tahunan

Menurut rilis resmi BPS Bali, jumlah wisatawan mancanegara yang tiba langsung di Bali pada April 2026 tercatat 553.328 kunjungan. Secara bulanan (month-on-month), angka ini meningkat 17,21 persen dibandingkan Maret 2026 — sinyal pemulihan musiman yang lazim terjadi menjelang musim panas belahan bumi utara.
